verses.text: Uzziah is made king at sixteen and, taught to seek God by Zechariah, prospers: 'as long as he sought the LORD, God made him to prosper.' His early faith is the spring of his greatness. Prosperity follows the heart that seeks God, though the test comes when the seeking slackens.

Uzziah is made king at sixteen and, taught to seek God by Zechariah, prospers: 'as long as he sought the LORD, God made him to prosper.' His early faith is the spring of his greatness. Prosperity follows the heart that seeks God, though the test comes when the seeking slackens.

Uzziah is made king at sixteen and, taught to seek God by Zechariah, prospers: 'as long as he sought the LORD, God made him to prosper.' His early faith is the spring of his greatness. Prosperity follows the heart that seeks God, though the test comes when the seeking slackens.

Uzziah is made king at sixteen and, taught to seek God by Zechariah, prospers: 'as long as he sought the LORD, God made him to prosper.' His early faith is the spring of his greatness. Prosperity follows the heart that seeks God, though the test comes when the seeking slackens.

Uzziah wins wars against Philistines and Arabians, and his fame spreads as far as Egypt, for God helped him. Strength flows from the LORD's aid. Whatever victories we win, the honest heart traces them back to the God who gave the help.

Uzziah wins wars against Philistines and Arabians, and his fame spreads as far as Egypt, for God helped him. Strength flows from the LORD's aid. Whatever victories we win, the honest heart traces them back to the God who gave the help.

Uzziah wins wars against Philistines and Arabians, and his fame spreads as far as Egypt, for God helped him. Strength flows from the LORD's aid. Whatever victories we win, the honest heart traces them back to the God who gave the help.

He builds towers, digs wells, and loves the soil, tending vineyards and fields. The king delights in honest, fruitful work. Greatness need not despise humble labour; even a crown is well worn by one who values the ground and its keeping.

He builds towers, digs wells, and loves the soil, tending vineyards and fields. The king delights in honest, fruitful work. Greatness need not despise humble labour; even a crown is well worn by one who values the ground and its keeping.

Uzziah raises a great, well-ordered army of mighty men. His kingdom grows strong and ready. God's blessing equips His servant with the means to stand, and order in small things undergirds strength in great ones.

Uzziah raises a great, well-ordered army of mighty men. His kingdom grows strong and ready. God's blessing equips His servant with the means to stand, and order in small things undergirds strength in great ones.

Uzziah raises a great, well-ordered army of mighty men. His kingdom grows strong and ready. God's blessing equips His servant with the means to stand, and order in small things undergirds strength in great ones.

He arms his troops well and devises engines to shoot arrows and stones from the towers; 'his name spread far abroad; for he was marvellously helped, till he was strong.' That last phrase is a warning. The very help that made him strong became the strength that would undo him.

He arms his troops well and devises engines to shoot arrows and stones from the towers; 'his name spread far abroad; for he was marvellously helped, till he was strong.' That last phrase is a warning. The very help that made him strong became the strength that would undo him.

When he was strong, his heart was lifted up to his destruction, and he forced his way into the temple to burn incense, a priestly act forbidden him; eighty priests withstood him. Pride forgets its limits. Strength untempered by humility presumes on holy ground not its to tread.

When he was strong, his heart was lifted up to his destruction, and he forced his way into the temple to burn incense, a priestly act forbidden him; eighty priests withstood him. Pride forgets its limits. Strength untempered by humility presumes on holy ground not its to tread.

When he was strong, his heart was lifted up to his destruction, and he forced his way into the temple to burn incense, a priestly act forbidden him; eighty priests withstood him. Pride forgets its limits. Strength untempered by humility presumes on holy ground not its to tread.

Enraged at the priests, Uzziah is struck with leprosy in his forehead, thrust out, and dwells apart, a leper till death, cut off from the temple he had profaned. The proud intruder is shut out forever. Sin against God's order brings a lasting and public mark.

Enraged at the priests, Uzziah is struck with leprosy in his forehead, thrust out, and dwells apart, a leper till death, cut off from the temple he had profaned. The proud intruder is shut out forever. Sin against God's order brings a lasting and public mark.

Enraged at the priests, Uzziah is struck with leprosy in his forehead, thrust out, and dwells apart, a leper till death, cut off from the temple he had profaned. The proud intruder is shut out forever. Sin against God's order brings a lasting and public mark.

Uzziah dies and is buried in the field of the kings, not their tombs, 'for they said, He is a leper.' Even in death the shame clings. A long and glorious reign is stained at the last by one proud, unrepented sin.
